excerpt: Complete breakdown of Maverick Trading pricing — all plans, hidden fees, and how to save money.
content:
Maverick Trading Pricing Overview

Maverick Trading is a US-based proprietary trading firm founded in 1997, based in Draper, Utah. Unlike many modern prop trading platforms that use challenge-based models, Maverick operates on a mentorship-first, training-focused approach. The firm charges an upfront cost for its trader development program before you can access funded trading capital—a model designed for serious traders willing to invest in education.

The starting price is $2,500 per month for the primary Trader Training Program. This positions Maverick at the higher end of prop firm costs compared to many challenge-based competitors, but the company argues this investment pays for itself through structured education, ongoing mentorship, and the potential for up to 80% profit splits once you're funded.

All Maverick Trading Plans Compared

Maverick Trading does not use a traditional tiered pricing model like many SaaS platforms. Instead, they offer a single comprehensive program:

Plan Name Monthly Cost Key Features
Trader Training Program $2,500/mo
  • Mentorship and education curriculum
  • Paper trading and backtesting tools
  • Risk management and position sizing training
  • Performance analytics and journaling
  • Broker integration for live trading
  • Access to trading community
  • Path to funded trading capital (up to 80% profit split)

Maverick Trading's single-tier model reflects their philosophy: there's one path to success, and it requires commitment. You're not choosing between "basic" and "pro"—you're either in the program or you're not.

What's included in the $2,500/month fee:

  • Structured education curriculum covering options, equity trading, risk management, and trading psychology
  • One-on-one and group mentorship with experienced traders (not limited to evaluation period)
  • Paper trading access to practice before committing real capital
  • Backtesting tools to analyze historical trading strategies
  • Performance tracking and journaling to identify patterns and improve
  • Broker integration (typically Interactive Brokers or similar)
  • No time-limited evaluation period—you progress at your own pace
  • Path to funding once ready with 80% profit splits

Free Plan / Free Trial

Maverick Trading does not offer a free tier or free trial.

This is a significant differentiator. While many modern prop firms (like Funded Trader or FTMO) let you try their challenge for free or pay a small fee ($30–$300), Maverick requires the full $2,500/month commitment to access any resources. There's no way to "test drive" the platform or education content without paying.

Is this a deal-breaker? Not necessarily. The lack of a free trial reflects Maverick's philosophy: they want traders who are serious and committed. However, it does mean you're taking a financial risk upfront. Most traders should:

  • Contact Maverick directly for a consultation to ensure the program aligns with your goals
  • Research testimonials and reviews from past traders
  • Ensure you have sufficient capital ($2,500/month for multiple months)
  • Understand you may not be approved for funding even after paying

Hidden Costs and Fees

The $2,500/month covers the core program, but be aware of potential additional costs:

  • Broker fees and commissions when paper or live trading—not covered by Maverick
  • Data feeds (real-time market data may require separate subscriptions depending on your broker)
  • Exchange fees for options trading if applicable
  • Trading losses during training—if paper trading funds are exhausted, you may need to purchase additional training capital
  • Funding evaluation fees—once you meet training milestones, additional evaluation or certification may carry separate costs (not confirmed, but typical in the industry)
  • No refunds—check their refund policy carefully; most prop firms operate on a non-refundable basis

Unlike platform-based prop firms that charge per evaluation attempt ($50–$300), Maverick's model means you're paying monthly whether you're actively trading or taking a break. Budget for at least 2–3 months minimum if you're evaluating the program seriously.

Maverick Trading Pricing vs Competitors

How does $2,500/month compare to other prop firms? The answer depends on which competitors you're considering:

Firm Model Starting Cost Profit Split
Maverick Trading Mentorship + Training $2,500/mo Up to 80%
FTMO (approximate) Challenge-based $300–$500 per challenge 80–90%
Funded Trader (approximate) Challenge-based $50–$200 per challenge 70–80%
Topstep (approximate) Challenge-based $100–$300 per attempt Variable

Key differences:

  • Cheaper upfront: FTMO and Funded Trader charge per challenge attempt, so you can test for $300 or less. Maverick requires $2,500/month upfront.
  • Different model: Challenge firms test your ability to trade profitably under constraints. Maverick trains you before funding, prioritizing education over speed to capital.
  • Longer commitment: If you spend 3 months in Maverick's program, you've paid $7,500. A challenge trader could fail and restart for $500 total.
  • Better mentorship: Maverick includes ongoing education and mentorship. Challenge firms focus on evaluation, not hand-holding.

Note: Competitor pricing is approximate as of 2026. Confirm current rates directly with each firm.

Is Maverick Trading Worth the Price?

Maverick Trading is worth it if:

  • You're a beginner trader who wants structured education, not just capital access. The $2,500/month trains you, not tests you.
  • You trade options or equities and want mentorship from experienced professionals. Maverick's 25+ year track record shows they know these markets.
  • You prioritize long-term skill building over fast funding. If you plan to trade for decades, education is worth the cost.
  • You have sufficient capital to sustain 2–4 months of training before being approved for funding.
  • You want ongoing mentorship after funding. Many prop firms drop you once you're funded; Maverick continues supporting traders.

Maverick Trading is NOT worth it if:

  • You're a professional trader who just needs capital. Paying for education when you already know your edge is wasteful.
  • You need access to crypto, forex, or futures. Maverick only supports US equities and options.
  • You want fast capital access. Challenge-based firms can fund you in weeks; Maverick's training takes months.
  • You're budget-conscious. At $2,500/month, Maverick is expensive compared to $100–$300 challenge attempts.
  • You prefer modern fintech tools. Maverick lacks a mobile app and cutting-edge platform features compared to newer prop firms.

Best for: Beginner options traders and equity traders in the US who can afford the upfront investment and value education over speed to funding.

How to Save on Maverick Trading

Maverick Trading's pricing is relatively fixed, but consider these strategies:

  • Annual prepayment: Contact Maverick directly to ask about annual billing discounts. Some firms offer 5–15% off for paying a year upfront ($2,500/mo × 12 = $30,000, potentially reduced to $25,500–$28,500).
  • Referral bonuses: Ask if Maverick offers referral discounts for bringing in other traders.
  • Group discounts: If trading with a group or company, negotiate a bulk rate.
  • Start during a promotion: Monitor Maverick's website or contact their sales team about seasonal discounts or promotions.
  • Skip the first month: Some firms credit your first month's fee if you sign a longer commitment. Negotiate this upfront.
  • Reduce broker costs: While not a Maverick discount, ensure your broker (Interactive Brokers, etc.) offers competitive commission rates to minimize total trading costs.

Honest reality: Maverick's pricing isn't negotiable for most traders. The best way to "save money" is to ensure you're in the program for the shortest time possible by learning quickly and reaching funding thresholds efficiently.

Final Verdict

Maverick Trading's $2,500/month fee is a premium price tag, but it reflects the firm's commitment to education and mentorship. If you're a serious trader willing to invest in your development, it's reasonable. If you're shopping purely on price, challenge-based firms are cheaper to try.

For more detailed reviews and comparisons, see our full Maverick Trading review and prop firm comparisons on TradingToolsHub.
